reasoning: I am voting for Biswajit Das and Mamata. Yes, I have seen the troubles — Sandeshkhali is eighty kilometres away but it is in Bengal, and the SSC scam is real. These things are serious and I will watch if the courts do their job properly. But I remember 2019, and I remember what it felt like when the BJP campaign came to Bangaon talking about 'infiltrators' — when my own family, rooted here for four generations, suddenly felt like suspects in our own town. Mamata kept that poison out. My daughter's stipend still comes, my wife's health card still works, and I do not feel like a foreigner when I walk down the main bazaar. Yes, the Matuas got their CAA certificates this week, and perhaps that pleases them, but that same law is the one that marked Muslims as a category of suspicion in my own country. One small batch of certificates does not change what CAA made me fear. In Bengal, every party has its bad people — this is how it works — but Mamata has shielded me from the national fever. That shield is what I vote for, not because she is perfect, but because the alternative feels unsafe for a man like me.
primary_drivers: NRC protection and Mamata's shield against national Hindu-nationalist fever
2019 BJP campaign trauma and the fear of being marked as a suspect in his own country
Practical security: daughter's stipend, wife's health card, feeling safe in Bangaon
